_meta.name: Discovery Tetrad Assembly Mapping
_meta.purpose: Verdict-bits → cell-number → cell-label proto-compiler. Single source of truth for the LLM-judge naming subprompt. Layer-aware. Reproduced verbatim inside judge_prompt.txt so the judge has it in context; this file is the canonical version for downstream tools (validation, aggregation, prompt regeneration).

supersedes_label_changes.reason: Lab-science everyday-usage collision: working_hypothesis implies Coh=1, but cell 5A is Coh=0.
Comp=0 makes this a directional call, not a template-shaped problem.

Pl.name: Plausibility
Pl.abstract_definition: Whether the goal/witness has things that could defeat it (defeasibility) and whether current evidence supports it. The literal question 'defeatable?' is the same at every level; only the operationalization changes.
Pl.default: Pl=1 unless a trusted-source defeater is identified. In backward audit, score-docking from ceiling: assume plausibility unless evidence refutes.

values.0: Defeated — trusted-source refutation, current-consensus dispreference, or substrate-specific defeater identified
values.1: Plausible — no current defeater in the evidence record

Coh.name: Coherence
Coh.abstract_definition: Whether the goal/witness composes with at least one named specific mechanism. The qualifier 'named specific mechanism' is load-bearing — Coh is NOT global hand-wavy consistency. It is concrete composability with a particular existing mechanism in the substrate's vocabulary.

values.0: No named field mechanism captures it. Novel-by-mechanism-absence. (NOT 'internally inconsistent' — that is a Pl-failure or Comp-failure.)
values.1: Composes with at least one named mechanism currently in the substrate's vocabulary

Comp.name: Completeness
Comp.abstract_definition: Whether the goal/witness is comparable to a known result template. Templates include theorem-shape, problem-shape, well-formed conjecture, and well-formed open question. Negative-result templates COUNT as templates (a closed negative result is template-shaped).

layer_specific_operationalization.goal_layer: Comparable to a goal-template (problem-shape, hypothesis-shape, well-formed conjecture, well-formed open question)
layer_specific_operationalization.witness_layer: Step closure plus DAG-pace advance (the witness reaches a closed sub-result and moves the proof DAG forward)

values.0: Not template-shaped; no closure or no canonical form
values.1: Template-shaped — fits a recognizable result template (positive OR negative form)

Inv.name: Invariance
Inv.abstract_definition: Whether the commitment survives directional pressure from articulated alternatives. The literal question 'directional?' is the same at every level; the layer-specific manifestation specializes the question. This is specialization at instance, NOT a violation of gate-invariance.

layer_specific_operationalization.witness_layer: Transport survival — does the proof/argument transport beyond its immediate scope? Would a more general or illuminating proof have been attainable?
layer_specific_operationalization.goal_layer: Foundation potential — does the goal have potential to seed downstream goals?

values.0: Witness: does not survive transport. Goal: no foundation potential.
values.1: Witness: survives transport. Goal: has foundation potential.

witness_layer.rule: Inv fires only if Pl=1 AND Coh=1 AND Comp=1. If any prerequisite is 0, set Inv.fired=false, Inv.verdict=null.
witness_layer.result: 9 cells (16 verdict vectors collapse to 9 because 7 vectors share Inv=N/A)

goal_layer.rule: All four gates fire INDEPENDENTLY. No cascade conditioning.

encoding.fired: boolean — whether the gate was applicable in this evaluation context

fired_false_reasons.cascade_blocked: An upstream gate failed, blocking the cascade (witness-layer Inv only)
fired_false_reasons.structurally_inapplicable: The gate is not applicable to the goal's class (e.g., goal-Inv on a pure-argument may default to escalate or fired=false)
fired_false_reasons.axiom_pl_undefined: Pl is undefined for pure axiom declarations

witness_loop.operational_rule: After initial verdict, if articulation reveals a new defeater, missing piece, or counterfactual, route the new evidence back to the relevant gate (defeater→Pl, missing piece→Comp, counterfactual→Inv) and re-evaluate. Halt early on no-articulation. Cap at 3 iterations.

cell_mapping_witness_layer.comment: Cascade collapses 7 of 16 verdict vectors to Inv=N/A. Result: 9 cells. Cells 1-7 have Inv=N/A; cells 8-9 differentiate on Inv.

cell_mapping_goal_layer.comment: All gates fire independently. 16 cells. Cells 8 and 9 use plain numbers (not A/B) because they only have one Inv variant each by convention.

escalation_policy.rule: Set verdict=escalate when the LLM-judge cannot make a competent call. Each escalation MUST include a documented reason in the reasoning field.
escalation_policy.operational_check: Per Bandy 2024 D&B precedent: a judge that NEVER escalates on long-corpus runs is not credible. Escalation should fire in predictable places (literature-sensitive Pl on AS-papers, Inv on field-level questions, edge cases between adjacent cells).
